title: Intense Indian Potatoes Curry
description: A garlicky, crumbly one‑pot Punjabi style potato curry that is dairy‑free and packed with bold spices.
instructions: Wash and cut the potatoes into bite‑sized pieces; set aside.
In a small bowl, mix the minced garlic, grated ginger, garam masala, cumin, turmeric, chili powder, and a pinch of salt to form a spice paste.
Heat the oil in a large pot over medium heat and add the spice paste, cooking for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Stir in the potato pieces and tomato puree, coating them evenly, and cook for another 3 minutes.
Add enough water to barely cover the potatoes, bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Once the potatoes are cooked, use a wooden spoon to gently crumble them, creating a chunky texture.
Taste and adjust seasoning with more salt if needed.
Remove the pot from heat and stir in chopped coriander leaves for freshness.
Let the curry rest for 5 minutes so the flavors meld.
Serve hot over steamed rice or with flatbread.
